content.problemStatement:
Chef lives at position xxx on the xxx - axis.

Chef has 2y2y2y friends, each living at every integer point in the range:
[xxx − yyy, xxx + yyy] except the position xxx itself.

Chef wants to visit his friends, but his mother has placed a strict rule:
Chef is allowed to travel at most zzz units away from his home in either direction. This means Chef can only move within the interval [xxx − zzz, xxx + zzz].

Your task is to determine how many of Chef’s friends live within the range Chef is allowed to travel.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer
TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input.
The line contains three space-separated integers
xxx, yyy, and zzz —
denoting Chef’s position on the xxx-axis,
the friend range [x−y,x+y][x - y, x + y][x−y,x+y],
and the maximum distance Chef is allowed to travel.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer — the number of friends Chef can visit.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the 1st test case, chef's friends live at position [-2, 2] except 0 and chef can visit positions [-1, 1]. So he can visit 2 friends.
In the 4th test case, chef can only visit position 7. So he cannot travel to any of his friends.
